evidence.scientific_clarity: Four communities are visually distinct via color and force-directed layout separates clusters reasonably; node ID labels (font_size=6, white on colored nodes) are barely legible at small sizes.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype='none' set; PDF uses Type 3 (non-selectable) DejaVuSans, SVG text converted to paths — default matplotlib font unchanged.
evidence.palette: Custom 4-color categorical palette (red/blue/teal/yellow) avoids pure green/red directional misuse; under deuteranopia C0-red and C1-blue collapse to near-identical gray (~110,110), reducing distinguishability to 3 effective colors.
evidence.layout_density: 7×7 inch canvas with 30 nodes is well-proportioned; tight_layout applied; no overlapping labels observed; axis off removes wasted border space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and mentions the algorithm; legend is present and placed lower-right; no numerical claim in caption (e.g., modularity, edge count) that a reviewer would demand.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF fonts are Type 3 (bitmapped outlines), not TrueType embedded via fonttype=42; text is not selectable or searchable in the PDF; SVG also has zero text elements (all paths).
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (117 KB), figure.svg (50 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the algorithm and mentions cross-community edges, but provides no quantitative finding (e.g., number of nodes/edges, modularity score, fraction of cross-community edges) — reads as a description of method, not a scientific result.

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; figure.svg is a bonus deliverable.
Custom categorical palette with reduced edge alpha (0.25) and white node borders avoids hairball and keeps community structure readable.
overall_weaknesses: No pdf.fonttype=42 / svg.fonttype='none': text is non-selectable Type 3 in PDF and path-converted in SVG, failing editability requirements.
Caption lacks any quantitative claim or scientific finding, and the red (#E63946) / teal (#2A9D8F) pair collapses under deuteranopia simulation, reducing effective colorblind distinguishability.
